title: Typed Async Work, Platform APIs, and Cancellation · Contract Defense
summary: Coordinate public-data requests, cancellation, retries, and UI state across browser and server boundaries. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a typed cancellable data coordinator. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Model promise fulfillment values and rejection handling without pretending rejected types are encoded in Promise.
Predict async return inference and retain discriminated result relationships across awaits.
Propagate AbortSignal and distinguish cancellation, timeout, transport, parsing, and domain failures.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.typescript:
// typescript-basics cumulative strict TypeScript portfolio.
// Add one typed behavior, runtime boundary, changed case, and evidence return per activity.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Promise Types evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Promise Types assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Two typed fetches race for service 2; abort the stale request, validate unknown JSON, update an aria-live status only from the winning response, and distinguish AbortError from a schema failure.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Promise Types has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Promise Types precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Two typed fetches race for service 2; abort the stale request, validate unknown JSON, update an aria-live status only from the winning response, and distinguish AbortError from a schema failure.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Promise has a second generic parameter for its rejection type.

options.text: Declare the domain relationship and identify every untrusted runtime boundary
Infer or annotate the smallest sound type and validate unknown values before narrowing
Run strict diagnostics, inspect emitted JavaScript, and execute the changed case
Add compile-time rejection and runtime boundary evidence, then document remaining limits

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Platform Lib Types: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Two typed fetches race for service 4; abort the stale request, validate unknown JSON, update an aria-live status only from the winning response, and distinguish AbortError from a schema failure.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Select DOM, worker, iterable, and server type libraries that match the real runtime without leaking incompatible globals.
